nodes.title: CL:Foundations of PBE
nodes.description: Introduces the principles and practices of place-based education. Students learn to design place-based curriculum and educative materials from an interdisciplinary analysis of specific places. Cross-listed with: SEP 1610.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission Required; Priority registration for students enrolled in PBE certificate. Then open to Degree & PACE students; Cross listed with SEP 1610A

nodes.title: CL:Place-Based Educ Capstone
nodes.description: The capstone experience for undergraduate students participating in the Place-Based Education Certificate Program; provides a structured opportunity for students to engage in dialogue and critical reflection, and to design a robust PBE experience in collaboration with a community partner. Prerequisites: EDTE 1610 or NR 1610; one of CDAE 2020, EDEC 3810, EDEL 3570, EDML 3890, PRT 2490. Pre/Co-requisites: Minimum Junior standing or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing or Instructor permission.

nodes.title: SU:Social Educ &Social Studies
nodes.description: Social Studies is the study of how humans have interacted with their environment and one another over time. The goal of teachers is to prepare students to be contributing and engaged citizens. Course covers how to teach social studies to children in a way that prepares them to be active citizens in a democratic and sustainable society. Focuses on elementary social studies standards, content, strategies, and resources. Pre/co-requisites: Teacher Education:Elementary Education major; EDEL 3550, EDEL 3580.
nodes.other_requirements: Teacher Education:Elementary Education major; ELK6 3rd yr, 4th yr; Admit to Teacher Education; 2.75 overall GPA requirement; Concurrent with EDEL 3550 and EDEL 3580.

nodes.title: CL:Sustainable Community Dev
nodes.description: Introduction to perspectives and methods used to develop healthy communities that are economically, socially, and environmentally sustainable with rural and urban, US and international examples. Prerequisites: CDAE 1020, ENVS 1510, or Instructor permission. Cross-listed with: ENVS 2470.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: CL:Tchng to Make a Difference
nodes.description: Serves as an introduction to the field of education and how teaching can foster a more just and humane world.

nodes.title: Inquiry-Based Pedagogy
nodes.description: Examines content/methods for designing, implementing, and evaluating inquiry-based curriculum that is authentic and meaningful to students' lives. Students will construct a shared vision for high quality instruction and explore how integrating Next Generation Science and C3 Framework for Social Studies can provide rich opportunities for engaging students in interdisciplinary, inquiry investigations. Prerequisites: EDEC 2130, EDEC 2510, or Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Resilience-based Outdoor Educa
nodes.description: Builds on theories of experiential education and social-ecological systems resilience to provides students with knowledge and skills related to outdoor education. Students will learn resilience-based approaches to leading groups in outdoor settings. Includes core DEI content (both principles and practices) related to critical, indigenous, and decolonizing perspectives on outdoor education.
